# Token Compare

> Token Compare is a free, browser-based LLM token counter and comparator. Paste two pieces of text side by side and instantly see how each tokenizes under the cl100k_base encoding used by GPT-4, GPT-4 Turbo, GPT-3.5-turbo, and ChatGPT. No account or signup required. All processing runs client-side in the browser.

## What it does

- Count tokens, characters, words, and lines for any text in real time
- Compare two texts side by side with a visual bar showing which uses fewer tokens
- Visualize individual tokens with color-coded highlights (each color = one token)
- Measure token density (tokens per word) and token efficiency (unique tokens / total tokens)
- Instant results — no API calls, no data sent to any server

## Encoding

Token Compare uses **cl100k_base**, the Byte Pair Encoding (BPE) tokenizer used by:
- GPT-4, GPT-4 Turbo, GPT-4o (input)
- GPT-3.5-turbo
- ChatGPT (all versions)
- text-embedding-ada-002

Note: Claude (Anthropic), Gemini (Google), and Llama models use different tokenizers. Token counts for those models will differ from what Token Compare reports.

## Who it is for

- **Prompt engineers** comparing two phrasings to pick the more token-efficient version
- **Developers** estimating OpenAI API costs before running large batch jobs
- **Researchers** studying how different languages, scripts, and code styles tokenize under cl100k_base
- **Technical writers** drafting content intended for LLM context windows with strict token budgets
- **LLM fine-tuners** understanding token distribution in training datasets

## Pricing

Free. No account required. No rate limits. Runs entirely in the browser using the open-source gpt-tokenizer library.

## Technology

- Frontend: Vanilla JavaScript + Vite
- Tokenizer: gpt-tokenizer (https://github.com/niieani/gpt-tokenizer) — a pure JavaScript implementation of cl100k_base
- Hosting: Cloudflare Pages
- No backend, no database, no telemetry

## Key pages

- Tool (main app): https://token-compare.pages.dev/
- Blog index: https://token-compare.pages.dev/blog/
- What is LLM Tokenization?: https://token-compare.pages.dev/blog/what-is-llm-tokenization/
- How to Count Tokens for GPT-4 & ChatGPT: https://token-compare.pages.dev/blog/how-to-count-tokens-gpt4/
- How to Reduce Token Count in LLM Prompts: https://token-compare.pages.dev/blog/reduce-token-count-prompts/
